title: E. coli K12 DNA Extraction
author: Kenneth Schackart
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yrpfv5n
materials: Gently pipet until the cells are resuspended.

steps.title: Culture E
steps.instruction: Culture E. coli K12 in BHI broth overnight. 2 mg lyophilized E. coli K12 in 10 mL BHI broth.

steps.title: Add 1 mL cell suspension to 1
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL cell suspension to 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Add 600 µL of Nuclei Lysis Solution
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL of Nuclei Lysis Solution.

steps.title: Incubate at 80 °C on heating...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 80 °C on heating block for 00:05:00 to lyse the cells.

steps.title: Cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of Protein Precipitation...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of Protein Precipitation Solution to the RNase-treated cell lysate.

steps.title: Vortex vigorously at high speed for 00:00:20
steps.instruction: Vortex vigorously at high speed for 00:00:20 .

steps.title: Add 3 µL RNase Solution to the cell lysate
steps.instruction: Add 3 µL RNase Solution to the cell lysate.

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 to 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 to 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 00:05:00

steps.title: Cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant containing the DNA to a clean 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant containing the DNA to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube containing 600 µL isopropanol. Some supernatant may remain in the original tube conatining the protein pellet. Leave this residual to avoid contaminating the DNA solution with the precipitated protein.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: Gently mix by inversion until the...
steps.instruction: Gently mix by inversion until the thread-like strands of DNA form a visible mass.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Carefully pour off the supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Carefully pour off the supernatant and drain the tube on clean absorbent paper.

steps.title: Add 600 µL of 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 600 µL of 70% ethanol and gently invert the tube several times to wash the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13,000-16,000 × g for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Drain the tube on clean absorbent...
steps.instruction: Drain the tube on clean absorbent paper and allow to air-dry for 10-15 minutes.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of DNA rehydration solution to the tube
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of DNA rehydration solution to the tube.

steps.title: Rehydrate by incubating the solution overnight...
steps.instruction: Rehydrate by incubating the solution overnight at room temperature or 4 °C .

steps.title: Store DNA at 2 °C to 8 °C
steps.instruction: Store DNA at 2 °C to 8 °C .
